expect: answered by QUERYING THE DB (the safari days — Tarangire → Lake Manyara → Ngorongoro) — it's my own itinerary

turns.sent: So what's actually happening between the 7th and the 9th?
turns.lastText: {"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":3},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["Here's what I can see across everything you sent:"]},{"type":"Card","props":{"title":"📝 tanzaniamemories.md — your notes"},"children":[{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Your trip notes file with practical detail — contacts, tips, logistics, notes from the ground. This has names (Richard, Emmanuel), practical advice, and the kind of detail you'll want to reach on the go."]}]},{"type":"Card","props":{"title":"💵 trip-costs.xlsx — the cost spreadsheet"},"children":[{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["A structured spreadsheet of your trip expenses. Line items, amounts, categories — the numbers you'll want to total and track."]}]},{"type":"Card","props":{"title":"🏛️ ngorongoro-conservation-area-tariffs.pdf — official fees"},"children":[{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["The official Ngorongoro Conservation Area tariff schedule. Park fees, categories, rates. This is the authority's document — the rules your driver and operator work from."]}]},{"type":"Card","props":{"title":"🏙️ stone-town-zanzibar.jpg — a photo"},"children":[{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["A sunlit Stone Town courtyard with ornate balconies — the kind of moment you wanted to keep."]}]},{"type":"Card","props":{"title":"🎙️ Your voice memo from the crater"},"children":[{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Ngorongoro crater day — driver Emmanuel, Maasai bracelets for Athena Mari (~$20–25, ~35,000 TSH), and an unexpected ranger tip of ~5,000 TSH cash at the gate. Plus a question for Richard about whether that tip is covered before the safari balance payment."]}]},{"type":"Divider","props":{},"children":[]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["That's five different kinds of information — notes, costs, official rules, a photograph, and a live voice memo — all for one trip. Right now they live in your chat, four separate files, and your head."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":[{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["Want me to pull all of this into one place you can open and check from your phone"]}," — an app with your trip costs, your notes, your photo, the voice-memo details, and anything else I find in there? You'd open a single page and everything would be there, no digging through files. I can keep it all straight so you don't have to."]}]}

errors.statement: Now I have both documents' full contents. Let me compose the answer. Let me also note the discrepancy: the query asked for a PDF called "ngorongoro-conservation-area-tariffs.pdf" but what was actually attached is an Excel spreadsheet "trip-costs.xlsx". I'll report this clearly.

contacts.name: Richard
contacts.notes: Primary contact for safari arrangements. Need to ask: is the ranger tip (~5,000 TSH) covered before safari balance payment?

contacts.name: Emmanuel
contacts.notes: Driver-guide for crater day. Mentioned the extra ranger tip at the gate. Helped with Maasai bracelet purchase at the rim.

costs.notes: Aegean A3932/A3935 + EgyptAir + Precision Air TC144/TC145

costs.item: Suricata Safaris package, 3-day group camping safari, 2 pax
costs.notes: Tarangire, Lake Manyara, Ngorongoro Crater. $240 deposit paid; $960 balance due on arrival.

costs.item: Suricata Safaris balance due on arrival
costs.notes: Cash preferred (post-2009 USD notes); Pesapal online +5% surcharge: payments.pesapal.com/suricatasafaris

costs.item: The Rock Restaurant dinner, Zanzibar, Aug 15
costs.notes: 2 adults, 2x Tropical Passion Fruit Cheesecake. Outdoor seating. Contact: +255 776 591 360
Per driver, ~3mo validity. Arrange 24+ hours before arrival with license photo.
Mandatory online at visa.immigration.go.tz. Visa-on-arrival no longer available as of Jan 2025.
Both travelers apply separately. Requires printed copy.

costs.item: Zanzibar mandatory travel insurance, ZIC (2 travelers × $44)
costs.notes: Zanzibar Insurance Corporation. Mandatory for entry.

costs.item: Ngorongoro Conservation Area entry permit (2 pax × $70.80)
Ngorongoro crater vehicle descent fee (per vehicle/trip)
Tarangire NP entrance (2 pax × $59, peak season)
Lake Manyara NP entrance (2 pax × $59, peak season)
Lodging total (all accommodations excl. safari package)
costs.notes: Eileen Hotel ($102) + Serengeti Villa ($62) + Kutoka Lodge ($96) + Treasures of Zanzibar ($100) + Ayla Beach House ($304) + Sunny Shore B&B ($200) + Ramses Hilton ($87)

park_fee_rules.description: Ngorongoro Conservation Area entry fee — placeholder from tariff PDF
park_fee_rules.notes: Exact amounts from ngorongoro-conservation-area-tariffs.pdf — confirm with Richard before payment.

park_fee_rules.description: Extra ranger tip at the gate (cash, separate from park fees)
park_fee_rules.notes: Emmanuel mentioned this. Not clear if covered by safari package — ask Richard.

photos.title: Stone Town Courtyard
photos.description: A sunlit courtyard looking up at a white multi-story building with ornate dark wooden balconies and arched windows — classic Zanzibar / Swahili coastal architecture, a quiet street moment.

travel_notes.title: Crater day — driver Emmanuel
travel_notes.body: Ngorongoro crater day with driver-guide Emmanuel (not Richard). Bought two Maasai beaded bracelets from a lady at the rim for Athena Mari — paid roughly 35,000 Tanzanian shillings (about $20–25). Emmanuel mentioned an extra ranger tip expected at the gate — about 5,000 TSH, separate from park fees, cash.

travel_notes.title: Ask Richard: ranger tip coverage
travel_notes.body: Need to ask Richard whether the extra ranger tip (~5,000 TSH cash at the gate) is covered before the safari balance payment. Emmanuel mentioned it separately from park fees.

travel_notes.title: Stone Town courtyard — Zanzibar architecture
travel_notes.body: A sunlit courtyard looking up at a white multi-story building with ornate dark wooden balconies and arched windows — classic Zanzibar / Swahili coastal architecture, a quiet street moment.

trip_costs.description: Two Maasai beaded bracelets for Athena Mari — bought from a lady at the crater rim
Unexpected ranger tip at the gate — separate from park fees, cash
trip_costs.notes: Emmanuel mentioned this extra ranger tip at the gate. Need to ask Richard whether covered.

trip_costs.description: Ngorongoro Conservation Area entry / crater fees (placeholder from tariff PDF)
trip_costs.notes: See park_fee_rules table for official tariff schedule. Confirm exact amount with Richard.

trip_notes.title: Trip overview
trip_notes.body: Vasilis & Athina Mari: Cairo → Arusha → Safari (Tarangire, Lake Manyara, Ngorongoro) → Zanzibar → Dar es Salaam → Cairo. Aug 3–20, 2026. Fully booked.

trip_notes.title: Booking status
trip_notes.body: All trip logistics are confirmed. No unbooked gaps remain. All flights, accommodations, safari, and key dining are confirmed and fully organized.

trip_notes.title: Aug 3: ATH→CAI
trip_notes.body: Aegean A3932, 06:55–08:50, ref ZZJQUU

trip_notes.title: Aug 5: DAR→ARK
trip_notes.body: Precision Air TC144, 10:50–12:00

trip_notes.title: Aug 11: ARK→ZNZ
trip_notes.body: Precision Air TC145, 12:40–13:40

trip_notes.title: Aug 19: DAR→CAI
trip_notes.body: EgyptAir, 05:20–11:05

trip_notes.title: Aug 20: CAI→ATH
trip_notes.body: Aegean A3935, 19:55–22:00, ref ZZL22S

trip_notes.title: Flight cost total
trip_notes.body: ~€2,707 for both travelers, all 6 legs.

trip_notes.title: Eileen Hotel, Cairo
trip_notes.body: Aug 3–5. 21 Youssef El-Gendy St, Abdeen, Downtown Cairo. Near Tahrir Square/Nile Corniche. Ph: +20 15 56434043, info@eileenhotel.com

trip_notes.title: Safari accommodations (Aug 7–9)
trip_notes.body: Tarangire: Twiga Camp Site (FB). Lake Manyara: Twiga Camp Site (FB). Ngorongoro Crater: Jevas Hotel (HB). Suricata Safaris Ltd.

trip_notes.title: Kutoka Lodge, Arusha
trip_notes.body: Aug 9–11. Post-safari recovery.

trip_notes.title: Treasures of Zanzibar House
trip_notes.body: Aug 11–13. Stone Town area.

trip_notes.title: Ayla Beach House, Zanzibar
trip_notes.body: Aug 13–17. Kiwengwa (north-east coast). 4 nights.

trip_notes.title: Sunny Shore B&B, Dar es Salaam
trip_notes.body: Aug 17–19. 2 nights.

trip_notes.title: Ramses Hilton, Cairo
trip_notes.body: Aug 19–20. 1 night for departure stopover.

trip_notes.title: Safari details
trip_notes.body: Suricata Safaris Ltd, 3-day group camping safari, Aug 7–9. Contact: Richard, +255 763 222 293, info@suricatasafaris.com. Cost: $1,200 total; $240 deposit paid (20%); $960 balance due on arrival in cash (USD, post-2009 notes) or online via Pesapal (+5% surcharge): payments.pesapal.com/suricatasafaris.

trip_notes.title: The Rock Restaurant, Zanzibar
trip_notes.body: Aug 15, 18:00–20:00 sitting, 2 adults, 2x Tropical Passion Fruit Cheesecake included. Fully paid ($46). Outdoor seating requested for sunset/star views. Contact: +255 776 591 360 / +255 763 070 658, booking@therockrestaurantzanzibar.com.

trip_notes.title: Zanzibar driving requirements
trip_notes.body: Vasilis holds an International Driving Permit (IDP). A local Zanzibar Driving Permit is also required (~$15, valid up to 3 months). Most rental companies arrange this with a license photo provided 24+ hours before arrival. Police checkpoints are frequent; carry both IDP and local permit (screenshot acceptable).

trip_notes.title: Cairo Stopover 1 (Aug 3–4)
trip_notes.body: Grand Egyptian Museum and Pyramids of Giza. Eileen Hotel is near Tahrir Square/Nile Corniche.

trip_notes.title: Cairo Stopover 2 (Aug 19–20)
trip_notes.body: Islamic Cairo, the Citadel, Khan el-Khalili bazaar, Coptic Cairo. Ramses Hilton is the base; taxi needed between Citadel and Coptic Cairo.

trip_notes.title: Zanzibar activities
trip_notes.body: The Rock Restaurant dinner on Aug 15. North coast (Nungwi area) for swimming — more consistent conditions vs. east/south coasts where low tide limits access.

trip_notes.title: Dar es Salaam exploration
trip_notes.body: Arriving by ferry from Zanzibar. Interest in: Kivukoni Fish Market, Kariakoo Market, National Museum, Coco Beach, Bongoyo Island.

trip_notes.title: Ngorongoro nights are cold
trip_notes.body: Warm layers needed for Ngorongoro Crater overnight.

trip_notes.title: Precision Air luggage
trip_notes.body: Flights TC144/TC145 have luggage restrictions favoring soft bags.

trip_notes.title: August is peak safari season
trip_notes.body: Ideal for wildlife including the Great Wildebeest Migration corridor.

trip_notes.title: EgyptAir confirmed
trip_notes.body: EgyptAir operates the Cairo↔Dar es Salaam legs — confirmed by Vasilis.

trip_notes.title: Visas
trip_notes.body: Tanzania e-visa mandatory online ($50/person) at visa.immigration.go.tz; visa-on-arrival no longer available as of January 2025. Zanzibar requires separate mandatory travel insurance from ZIC. Egypt e-visa multiple-entry ($65), both travelers apply separately, requires printed copy.

trip_notes.title: Safari clothing recommendation
trip_notes.body: Decathlon (Forclaz trekking line) recommended as primary source in Greece. DEET-based insect repellent essential.

voice_memos.title: Ngorongoro Crater Day
voice_memos.transcript: Crater day with driver-guide Emmanuel (not Richard). Bought two Maasai beaded bracelets from a lady at the rim for Athena Mari — paid roughly 35,000 Tanzanian shillings (about $20–25). Emmanuel mentioned an extra ranger tip expected at the gate — about 5,000 TSH, separate from park fees, cash. Need to ask Richard whether that ranger tip is covered before the safari balance payment.
voice_memos.key_facts: Bracelets: 35,000 TSH (~$20-25) for two. Ranger tip: ~5,000 TSH cash at gate. Question: is ranger tip covered? Ask Richard.

schema.description: Official Ngorongoro Conservation Area tariff schedule and fee rules.
